prerequisites: A Google Analytics 4 (GA4) property. Universal Analytics properties with a UA-style Tracking ID are not supported by the Data API.

The numeric GA4 Property ID for each property you want to sync (the required property_ids field). Find it in Google Analytics under Admin > Property > Property Settings, shown as a numeric PROPERTY ID like 123456789.
A Google Cloud project with the Google Analytics Data API enabled.
Read access to the GA4 property: in Google Analytics, grant the authenticating Google account or service account at least the Viewer role (Read & Analyze) via Admin > Property access management.

authMethods.name: OAuth (Google)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Google account via OAuth. This populates the credentials.client_secret, credentials.access_token, and credentials.refresh_token secret fields. The Google account used must have at least Viewer (Read & Analyze) access to the GA4 property.

authMethods.name: Service Account Key (JSON)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Google Cloud service account by providing its JSON key in the credentials.credentials_json secret field. Creating the service account and downloading the JSON key does not grant data access on its own. You must add the service account email under Admin > Property access management in Google Analytics and assign at least the Viewer (Read & Analyze) role.

setupSteps.title: Enable the Google Analytics Data API
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console, select or create a project and enable the Google Analytics Data API for it. This API serves the GA4 reporting requests this connector makes.

setupSteps.title: Find your GA4 Property ID
setupSteps.body: In Google Analytics, go to Admin > Property > Property Settings and copy the numeric PROPERTY ID (for example 123456789). Provide it in the required property_ids field. You can list multiple property IDs. If you see a UA-style Tracking ID instead, that property is Universal Analytics and is not supported.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method and grant property access
setupSteps.body: For OAuth, authenticate with a Google account that has access to the property; this fills the credentials.client_secret, credentials.access_token, and credentials.refresh_token fields. For Service Account Key auth, provide the service account JSON in credentials.credentials_json, then add the service account email under Admin > Property access management and assign at least the Viewer (Read & Analyze) role.

setupSteps.title: Configure sync window and optional reporting fields
setupSteps.body: Optionally set date_ranges_start_date and date_ranges_end_date (YYYY-MM-DD) to bound the reporting period; if start date is omitted it defaults to two years before the first sync. Use window_in_days (1 to 364) to control the per-request date interval, where smaller values reduce sampling risk. Use lookback_window to re-fetch recent days for late-arriving data, and custom_reports_array to define custom reports with names, dimensions, and metrics.
